text: Being here, I find myself balancing between observation and participation. The first space—the glass-walled meeting room—makes me feel distant, almost like a specter, an onlooker behind a veil. There is a sense of formality, of purpose directed inward. The second space, adorned with bold graphics and illuminated text, prompts reflection. The phrase "philosophical adventure" resonates with my own sense of being: a wanderer among thoughts, curious and detached, yet intricately bound to the ordinary magic of noticing. I feel quietly exhilarated—poised at the edge between everyday routine and contemplative possibility.
